name: Ecobank Pan-African Digital Banking and Payments API
what_it_does: Ecobank pan-African digital banking and payments REST API for African businesses, fintechs, and enterprises to access banking services across 33 African countries through Ecobank's pan-African banking network — enabling cross-border payment initiation, account-to-account transfers, mobile money integration, account balance inquiry, statement retrieval, and merchant payment processing across West, Central, East, and Southern Africa through Africa's largest banking network by country coverage. Enables AI agents to manage cross-border transfer for pan-African payment automation, handle account inquiry for African bank account verification automation, access payment processing for African merchant payment automation, retrieve account statement for African banking reconciliation automation, manage mobile money for African wallet integration automation, handle SWIFT for international wire automation, access card payment for African card acceptance automation, retrieve FX rates for African currency conversion automation, manage bulk payment for African payroll and disbursement automation, and integrate Ecobank with African enterprise, government, and NGO financial operations for pan-African banking automation.
use_cases: Initiating cross-border payments across 33 African countries through a single Ecobank API integration automation agents
Verifying African bank account details and balances across Ecobank's pan-African network for payment verification automation agents
Processing bulk payroll and disbursements to African employees and beneficiaries across multiple countries automation agents
Integrating Ecobank with African NGO and multinational enterprise financial operations for multi-country banking automation agents
not_for: Outside Africa (Ecobank focuses on 33 African countries)
Consumer retail banking (Ecobank API is for business and enterprise clients)
Real-time high-frequency trading (Ecobank is traditional banking, not fintech infrastructure)
best_when: A multinational African enterprise, NGO, or fintech wanting AI agents to process cross-border payments, manage accounts, and automate banking operations across 33 African countries through a single pan-African banking API.
avoid_when: CORPORATE BANKING RELATIONSHIP REQUIRED: Ecobank API requires established corporate banking relationship; automated self-service signup assumption creates no API access; automated must have active Ecobank corporate account in at least one Ecobank country. REGULATORY PATCHWORK ACROSS 33 COUNTRIES: Ecobank operates under different central bank regulations in each of its 33 African countries; automated unified regulatory framework assumption creates country-specific compliance gaps; automated must understand country-specific regulations for each operation. CROSS-BORDER FX CONTROLS: Many African countries have currency controls and FX restrictions; automated unrestricted cross-border transfer assumption creates regulatory violation in countries with FX controls (e.g., Nigeria CBN FX restrictions); automated must comply with each country's FX regulations. API CAPABILITIES VARY BY COUNTRY: Not all Ecobank API products are available in all 33 countries; automated uniform API coverage assumption creates feature_not_available in some markets; automated must test country-specific availability.

auth.notes: Ecobank uses API key + OAuth2 for enterprise API authentication. REST API with JSON. Lomé, Togo HQ (Group) with key operations in Lagos, Accra, Nairobi. Founded 1985. Listed on Ghana Stock Exchange, Nigeria Stock Exchange. Products: Cross-border payment, account services, mobile banking, corporate banking, FX, trade finance, merchant payment. SDKs: None public. 33 African countries. Largest pan-African bank by country coverage. Competes with Standard Bank and UBA for pan-African banking services.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox for approved corporate partners; production requires corporate banking relationship
pricing.paid_tiers: Transaction fees negotiated per corporate banking agreement; FX spread on cross-border; SWIFT fees
pricing.notes: Lomé TG. Multi-country central bank regulation. Per-transaction negotiated. 33 African countries. Corporate banking.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API client; cross-border and payment APIs throttled per corporate agreement

known_agent_gotchas: CORPORATE ACCOUNT MANDATORY: Ecobank API requires existing corporate banking client relationship; automated public developer signup assumption creates no API access; automated must establish Ecobank corporate banking relationship before API development

COUNTRY-SPECIFIC API AVAILABILITY: Not all Ecobank API products are available in all 33 African countries; automated uniform coverage assumption creates feature_not_available; automated must verify specific product availability per country before integration

AFRICAN FX CONTROLS: Multiple Ecobank countries have currency controls (Nigeria, Ethiopia, Zimbabwe, etc.); automated unrestricted cross-border transfer assumption creates regulatory violation; automated must comply with each country's FX regulations and limits

CROSS-BORDER SETTLEMENT IS NOT INSTANT: Cross-border payments across Ecobank network may take 1-3 days; automated instant cross-border assumption creates delayed payment processing; automated must implement payment status polling for cross-border settlement confirmation

DOCUMENTATION IS UNEVENLY MAINTAINED: Ecobank developer documentation varies in completeness by API product; automated comprehensive documentation assumption creates undocumented behavior discovery; automated should plan for direct Ecobank integration support during development
agent_readiness.security_notes: Pan-African banking. Multi-country central bank regulation. API key + OAuth2. 33 African countries. Corporate banking and cross-border payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Pan-African bank. Multi-country central bank. API key + OAuth2. Africa. Corporate and cross-border payment data.
